provisions.description: Requires the Inspectors General of the Department of Commerce, Department of Justice, NASA, NSF, and the Legal Services Corporation to conduct audits of grants or contracts funded by this Act. Reports on audit progress are required every 180 days. Audit results must be made publicly available within 60 days of completion, with redactions for FOIA exemptions and sensitive personal information. Grant and contract recipients must certify no subcontract funds go to persons with a financial interest in the awardee.
notes: Subsection (a): IG audits pursuant to Inspector General Act (5 U.S.C. App.)
Subsection (b): Results must be posted on agency websites in redacted form
Subsection (c): Certification requirement for grant/contract awardees regarding subcontract financial interests
Subsection (d): Provisions take effect 30 days after OMB Director (consulting with OGE Director) determines uniform ethics rules substantially similar to these apply across executive branch

cross_references.target: NIST Federal Information Processing Standard Publication 199

provisions.description: Prohibits use of funds by the Departments of Commerce and Justice, NASA, or NSF to acquire a high-impact or moderate-impact information system (per NIST FIPS 199) unless the agency has (1) reviewed supply chain risk against NIST/FBI criteria, (2) reviewed presumptive awardee against FBI threat information, and (3) conducted a cyber-espionage/sabotage risk assessment including risks from entities owned, directed, or subsidized by China, Iran, North Korea, or Russia. Acquisition prohibited unless the head of the assessing entity has developed a mitigation strategy, determined the acquisition is in the national interest, and reported to Appropriations Committees and the agency IG.
notes: Subsection (a): Three-part pre-acquisition review requirement
Subsection (b): Three conditions for proceeding with acquisition after review
Specifically names PRC, Iran, DPRK, and Russia as cyber threat sources
References NIST FIPS 199 security categorization standard

provisions.description: Restricts reprogramming of Title I funds with detailed thresholds by category (Investigations, Construction, O&M, MRT, FUSRAP), requires prior approval from Appropriations Committees for certain changes, establishes $50,000 de minimis threshold, and exempts continuing authorities program from new-program prohibition.
notes: Subsection (a)(1)-(5): Requires prior Committee approval for creating/eliminating programs, increasing denied/restricted programs, or redirecting directed funds
Subsection (a)(6) Investigations: 25% up to $150,000 for base >$100,000; $25,000 for base <$100,000; $25,000 into unfunded continuing studies

Subsection (a)(7) Construction: 15% up to $3,000,000 for base >$2,000,000; $300,000 for base <$2,000,000; up to $3,000,000 for settled claims/changed conditions; $300,000 into unfunded continuing studies
Subsection (a)(8) O&M: Unlimited for emergencies with post-notification; 15% up to $5,000,000 for base >$1,000,000; $150,000 for base <$1,000,000; $150,000 into unfunded continuing studies
Subsection (a)(9) MRT: Same guidelines as (6)-(8) for respective Investigations/Construction/O&M portions
Subsection (a)(10) FUSRAP: 15% of receiving project base
Subsection (b): De minimis threshold—no reprogramming submission for less than $50,000
Subsection (c): Continuing authorities program exempted from (a)(1)

Establishes billing and payment schedules.

metadata.agency: Department of the Interior

notes: Heading: OUTER CONTINENTAL SHELF INSPECTION FEES
Subsection (b) annual facility fees (excluding drilling rigs): (1) $10,500 for facilities with no wells but processing/gathering lines; (2) $17,000 for 1-10 wells; (3) $31,500 for >10 wells
Subsection (c) drilling rig inspection fees: (1) $30,500 for ≥500 ft water depth; (2) $16,700 for <500 ft
Subsection (d) non-rig unit inspection fees per 30 CFR 250 subparts D, E, F, Q: (1) $13,260 for ≥2,500 ft; (2) $11,530 for 500–2,499 ft; (3) $4,470 for <500 ft
Subsection (e) billing: quarterly for facilities; within 30 days of month-end for rigs; by end of following quarter for non-rig units; 30 days payment window

provisions.description: Requires the Secretary of the Interior to allocate National Parks and Public Land Legacy Restoration Fund and Land and Water Conservation Fund amounts within 45 days of enactment per specified tables in the explanatory statement. Limits presidential reallocation authority. Establishes procedures for contingency fund reallocation. Requires budget submissions with project data sheets and supplementary allocation lists. Mandates quarterly reports on fund balances to Appropriations Committees.
notes: Subsection (a)(1): Allocation of NPPLLRF funds per explanatory statement table
Subsection (a)(2): Allocation of LWCF funds per explanatory statement table
Subsection (b): Limits presidential reallocation; continuing admin may be allocated at no more than current levels
Subsection (c): Contingency fund reallocation for unforeseen cost overruns within original project scope; 30-day notice required for reallocations 10% or more above allocated or estimated amounts
Subsection (d)(1): Requires project data sheets concurrent with FY2025 budget including contingency fund line
Subsection (d)(2): Requires supplementary allocation lists for federal land acquisition and Forest Legacy Projects
Subsection (e): Quarterly reports on status of NPPLLRF and LWCF project balances

provisions.description: Establishes the distribution methodology for the obligation limitation for Federal-aid highways for FY2024, including exceptions from the limitation, redistribution of unused obligation authority, applicability to transportation research programs, and redistribution of certain authorized funds.
notes: Standard annual obligation limitation distribution formula for Federal-aid highways
Subsection (a) establishes 5-step process for distributing obligation limitation among states and programs
Subsection (b) lists 12 categories of exceptions from the obligation limitation, including $639,000,000 exemptions for section 119 of title 23 (b)(12) for each of FY2013-2024
Subsection (c) requires redistribution of unused obligation authority after August 1
Subsection (d) applies obligation limitation to transportation research programs under chapter 5 of title 23, title VI of FAST Act, and title III of IIJA, with 4-year availability
Subsection (e) requires redistribution within 30 days of funds that will not be allocated due to obligation limitation

provisions.description: Prohibits Section 8 housing assistance for individuals who meet ALL of the following: enrolled at institution of higher education, under 24, not a veteran, unmarried, no dependent child, not a person with disabilities (or was not receiving Section 8 as of November 30, 2005), not a youth who left foster care at age 14+ and at risk of homelessness, and not otherwise individually eligible (or whose parents are not eligible). Also requires that financial assistance received under the Higher Education Act (in excess of tuition and required fees) be counted as income for Section 8 eligibility, except for persons over 23 with dependent children.
notes: Subsection (a) lists 8 conditions that must ALL be met for the prohibition to apply — individual must satisfy every criterion (1)-(8)

provisions.description: Amends the Rental Assistance Demonstration (RAD) authority in the HUD Appropriations Act, 2012 (title II of division C of P.L. 112-55) in multiple ways: (1) adds 'Public Housing Fund' as eligible funding source; (2) extends deadline from 2024 to 2029; (3) adds provisos allowing conversion of tenant protection vouchers to project-based subsidy contracts at properties with partial section 9 conversion and section 18 events, with authority to transfer corresponding funds; (4) adds authority for HUD to consolidate project-based subsidy contracts with section 8(o)(13) assistance; (5) adds eligible fund headings including 'Self-Sufficiency Programs' and 'Family Self-Sufficiency'; (6) extends eligibility to owners with senior preservation rental assistance contracts under section 811 of the American Homeownership and Economic Opportunity Act of 2000; (7) updates references to conversion of assistance types.
notes: Seven subsections amending the RAD authority originally enacted in P.L. 112-55
Paragraph (1): Adds Public Housing Fund as eligible source
Paragraph (2): Extends RAD deadline from 2024 to 2029
Paragraph (3): Adds new provisos for partial conversions, TPV-to-PBRA transfers, and contract consolidation
Paragraph (4): Adds Public Housing Fund, Self-Sufficiency Programs, and Family Self-Sufficiency headings; adds services availability criterion
Paragraph (5): Extends eligibility to senior preservation rental assistance contracts under section 811 of AHEOA 2000
Paragraph (6): Adds section 811 of AHEOA 2000 to eligible conversion authorities
Paragraph (7): Updates references to conversion of section 202(c)(2), section 811 AHEOA 2000, and section 811(d)(2) Cranston-Gonzalez

provisions.description: Restricts reprogramming of funds provided in this Act, by previous appropriations Acts, or from fee-derived accounts, unless prior approval is received from House and Senate Appropriations Committees. Reprogramming is prohibited if it: (1) creates a new program; (2) eliminates a program/project/activity; (3) increases funds/personnel for items denied or restricted by Congress; (4) uses directed funds for different purposes; (5) augments existing programs by more than $5,000,000 or 10% (whichever is less); (6) reduces existing programs by $5,000,000 or 10% (whichever is less); or (7) creates/reorganizes organizational units beyond what was submitted in budget justifications.
notes: Includes proviso requiring each agency to submit a reprogramming baseline report within 60 days of enactment

cross_references.target: Title XIX of the Social Security Act (42 U.S.C. 1396 et seq.)

deadlines: 18 months after enactment for initial analysis
January 1 of each calendar year for annual updates

provisions.description: Requires CMS to collect, link, analyze, and publish annually on a public website de-identified T-MSIS data on substance use disorder and mental health services provided to Medicaid and CHIP enrollees, disaggregated by age. Establishes detailed data requirements including enrollment categories, treatment types and settings, managed care vs. fee-for-service breakdowns, readmissions, emergency department visits, assessments, and prescription data. First analysis due within 18 months of enactment; annual updates by January 1 each year; revised research-ready publication within 3 years. Requires system of records notice and data-sharing activities initiated by January 1, 2025.
notes: Adds new Section 1948 to Title XIX of the Social Security Act.
Very detailed data collection and reporting requirements for Medicaid/CHIP behavioral health data.
Under Subtitle B—Medicaid

cross_references.description: Definition of combat-related for research purposes

cross_references.target: Animal Welfare Act
Public Health Service Policy on Humane Care and Use of Laboratory Animals

provisions.description: Prohibits use of funds for research using canines, felines, or non-human primates unless the Secretary of VA personally certifies in writing that no alternatives exist, the objectives are combat-related, and ethical considerations are met. Requires extensive reporting on any approved research. Requires VA to submit to voluntary USDA inspections. Mandates elimination of canine, feline, and non-human primate research within 2 years of enactment.
notes: Subsection (a): General prohibition on canine, feline, and non-human primate research without Secretary's approval

Subsection (b): Secretary may approve only if (A) no alternatives, (B) combat-related per 10 U.S.C. 1413(e), (C) consistent with Dec 15, 2017 VA canine research policy, (D) ethical considerations included. Authority may not be delegated.
Subsection (c): 30-day advance report to Appropriations Committees before commencement of any approved new research
Subsection (d): Biannual report starting 180 days after enactment on all ongoing research using these animals
Subsection (e): Annual submission to voluntary USDA inspections starting 180 days after enactment
Subsection (f): Annual report on Animal Welfare Act violations, corrective actions, and prevention measures
Subsection (g): Mandate to eliminate all canine, feline, and non-human primate research within 2 years of enactment
